FAQ
For other questions, contact Lilly Gullickson at lgullickson@uchicago.edu.
[Application](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[When is the application due?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The summer 2026 application is due on January 12, 2025 at 11:59pm CT. [Where can I apply?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Check out our application page [here](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/internships/application). [When will I be notified of my application decision?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Please subscribe to the [DSI Mailing List](https://mailchi.mp/6fb19adbafc8/data-science-institute) to receive notifications about the 2026 program and application. We expect to inform applicants about their decision in March 2026. [Are letters of recommendation required?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We do not require letters of recommendation, but you may submit an optional letter of recommendation for the Summer Lab application. The letter can come from a teacher, a coach, employer, or mentor and should speak to your enthusiasm about learning, research, and/or community. The recommender must submit their letter through a form; the letter submission form is linked in the Summer Lab application. Letters are due at the same time as Summer Lab applications: January 12 at 11:59 CT.
[When is the application due?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The summer 2026 application is due on January 12, 2025 at 11:59pm CT.

We expect to inform applicants about their decision in March 2026.

[Are letters of recommendation required?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We do not require letters of recommendation, but you may submit an optional letter of recommendation for the Summer Lab application. The letter can come from a teacher, a coach, employer, or mentor and should speak to your enthusiasm about learning, research, and/or community. The recommender must submit their letter through a form; the letter submission form is linked in the Summer Lab application. Letters are due at the same time as Summer Lab applications: January 12 at 11:59 CT.
We do not require letters of recommendation, but you may submit an optional letter of recommendation for the Summer Lab application. The letter can come from a teacher, a coach, employer, or mentor and should speak to your enthusiasm about learning, research, and/or community. The recommender must submit their letter through a form; the letter submission form is linked in the Summer Lab application. Letters are due at the same time as Summer Lab applications: January 12 at 11:59 CT.
[Eligibility](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[Are there any program prerequisites?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We do not require any previous research experience to participate in the program. Familiarity in at least one programming language (Python, Java, C++, etc.) is preferred, as well as relevant coursework in areas such as computer science, statistics, and math. [What grade or age of students are eligible to apply?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Applicants may be in high school or college. High school applicants must be located in the Chicago area and must be able to comfortably commute to the UChicago Hyde Park campus. Undergraduate applicants must be enrolled in college in fall 2026 (graduating college seniors are not eligible). Current high school seniors who will be starting college in fall 2026 will be categorized as high school applicants and therefore must be located in the Chicago area. Graduate students are not eligible. [Are international students eligible to apply?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Yes, international undergraduate students are eligible to apply. However, all students must be authorized to work in the United States and provide all necessary documentation in support of their stipend. To see the documentation required to process stipends, please consult [this page](https://finserv.uchicago.edu/payroll/department/stipend.shtml). We recommend that all international students check with their home institution’s international affairs office to ensure that they qualify.

Familiarity in at least one programming language (Python, Java, C++, etc.) is preferred, as well as relevant coursework in areas such as computer science, statistics, and math.

Graduate students are not eligible.

[Program Details](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[What are the stipend rates for the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The stipend rate for the 8-week 2026 program will be $5600. [How many hours a week is the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)While students are not required to log their hours, we expect each student to work a full-time schedule each week from 9am – 5pm. [Is housing provided?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We provide optional housing for college students as part of this program free of charge. We are not able to provide housing to minors, and high school students of any age (including current high school seniors) are responsible for arranging their own housing and transportation. [Will the 2025 program be in person?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The 2026 program will take place in person at the UChicago Hyde Park campus. [What are the 2026 program dates?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The 2026 program dates are June 15th – August 7th, 2026.
[What are the stipend rates for the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The stipend rate for the 8-week 2026 program will be $5600.

[Is housing provided?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We provide optional housing for college students as part of this program free of charge. We are not able to provide housing to minors, and high school students of any age (including current high school seniors) are responsible for arranging their own housing and transportation.
We provide optional housing for college students as part of this program free of charge. We are not able to provide housing to minors, and high school students of any age (including current high school seniors) are responsible for arranging their own housing and transportation.
[Will the 2025 program be in person?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The 2026 program will take place in person at the UChicago Hyde Park campus.
The 2026 program will take place in person at the UChicago Hyde Park campus.
[What are the 2026 program dates?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The 2026 program dates are June 15th – August 7th, 2026.
The 2026 program dates are June 15th – August 7th, 2026.
[Projects](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[Where can I see past projects in the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)You can view profiles of past projects in the program on our [Alumni and Projects page](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/alumni-and-projects). Profiles include details about the student’s mentor, a description of the project, and their final poster. [If admitted, how will I be paired with a project?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)During the application review process, we use your written statements, experience, and research keywords to match applicants with mentors and projects based on shared interests. We aim to build a cohort with a variety of levels of experience with research and data science. Admitted students will be informed of their mentor and project match at the time of admission notification.
[Where can I see past projects in the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)You can view profiles of past projects in the program on our [Alumni and Projects page](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/alumni-and-projects). Profiles include details about the student’s mentor, a description of the project, and their final poster.
You can view profiles of past projects in the program on our [Alumni and Projects page](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/alumni-and-projects). Profiles include details about the student’s mentor, a description of the project, and their final poster.
[If admitted, how will I be paired with a project?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)During the application review process, we use your written statements, experience, and research keywords to match applicants with mentors and projects based on shared interests. We aim to build a cohort with a variety of levels of experience with research and data science. Admitted students will be informed of their mentor and project match at the time of admission notification.
During the application review process, we use your written statements, experience, and research keywords to match applicants with mentors and projects based on shared interests. We aim to build a cohort with a variety of levels of experience with research and data science.
Admitted students will be informed of their mentor and project match at the time of admission notification.

# FAQ
#### For other questions, contact Lilly Gullickson at lgullickson@uchicago.edu.
- [Application](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[When is the application due?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The summer 2026 application is due on January 12, 2025 at 11:59pm CT. [Where can I apply?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Check out our application page [here](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/internships/application). [When will I be notified of my application decision?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Please subscribe to the [DSI Mailing List](https://mailchi.mp/6fb19adbafc8/data-science-institute) to receive notifications about the 2026 program and application. We expect to inform applicants about their decision in March 2026. [Are letters of recommendation required?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We do not require letters of recommendation, but you may submit an optional letter of recommendation for the Summer Lab application. The letter can come from a teacher, a coach, employer, or mentor and should speak to your enthusiasm about learning, research, and/or community. The recommender must submit their letter through a form; the letter submission form is linked in the Summer Lab application. Letters are due at the same time as Summer Lab applications: January 12 at 11:59 CT.
- [When is the application due?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The summer 2026 application is due on January 12, 2025 at 11:59pm CT.

We expect to inform applicants about their decision in March 2026.

The letter can come from a teacher, a coach, employer, or mentor and should speak to your enthusiasm about learning, research, and/or community. The recommender must submit their letter through a form; the letter submission form is linked in the Summer Lab application. Letters are due at the same time as Summer Lab applications: January 12 at 11:59 CT.
We do not require letters of recommendation, but you may submit an optional letter of recommendation for the Summer Lab application. The letter can come from a teacher, a coach, employer, or mentor and should speak to your enthusiasm about learning, research, and/or community. The recommender must submit their letter through a form; the letter submission form is linked in the Summer Lab application. Letters are due at the same time as Summer Lab applications: January 12 at 11:59 CT.
- [Eligibility](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[Are there any program prerequisites?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We do not require any previous research experience to participate in the program. Familiarity in at least one programming language (Python, Java, C++, etc.) is preferred, as well as relevant coursework in areas such as computer science, statistics, and math. [What grade or age of students are eligible to apply?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Applicants may be in high school or college. High school applicants must be located in the Chicago area and must be able to comfortably commute to the UChicago Hyde Park campus. Undergraduate applicants must be enrolled in college in fall 2026 (graduating college seniors are not eligible). Current high school seniors who will be starting college in fall 2026 will be categorized as high school applicants and therefore must be located in the Chicago area. Graduate students are not eligible. [Are international students eligible to apply?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)Yes, international undergraduate students are eligible to apply. However, all students must be authorized to work in the United States and provide all necessary documentation in support of their stipend. To see the documentation required to process stipends, please consult [this page](https://finserv.uchicago.edu/payroll/department/stipend.shtml). We recommend that all international students check with their home institution’s international affairs office to ensure that they qualify.

Familiarity in at least one programming language (Python, Java, C++, etc.) is preferred, as well as relevant coursework in areas such as computer science, statistics, and math.

- [Program Details](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[What are the stipend rates for the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The stipend rate for the 8-week 2026 program will be $5600. [How many hours a week is the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)While students are not required to log their hours, we expect each student to work a full-time schedule each week from 9am – 5pm. [Is housing provided?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)We provide optional housing for college students as part of this program free of charge. We are not able to provide housing to minors, and high school students of any age (including current high school seniors) are responsible for arranging their own housing and transportation. [Will the 2025 program be in person?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The 2026 program will take place in person at the UChicago Hyde Park campus. [What are the 2026 program dates?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)The 2026 program dates are June 15th – August 7th, 2026.

- [Projects](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)[Where can I see past projects in the program?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)You can view profiles of past projects in the program on our [Alumni and Projects page](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/alumni-and-projects). Profiles include details about the student’s mentor, a description of the project, and their final poster. [If admitted, how will I be paired with a project?](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/summerlab/faq)During the application review process, we use your written statements, experience, and research keywords to match applicants with mentors and projects based on shared interests. We aim to build a cohort with a variety of levels of experience with research and data science. Admitted students will be informed of their mentor and project match at the time of admission notification.

Profiles include details about the student’s mentor, a description of the project, and their final poster.

We aim to build a cohort with a variety of levels of experience with research and data science. Admitted students will be informed of their mentor and project match at the time of admission notification.
